description: European Commission reduced 2026 steel import quotas by 3% across product categories. Hot-rolled coil prices up 8% in Q1. SKF's European bearing plants face rising input costs for ring and roller production.
skf_exposure: Direct cost impact on Gothenburg, Landskrona, Bietigheim, Steyr ring production. Steel is SKF's #1 input cost.
recommended_action: Accelerate dual-sourcing from Indian and Turkish steel mills. Review Q2 pricing pass-through with OEM customers.

actions.action: Negotiate forward contracts with Indian steel mills (JSW, Tata) for Q2-Q3 ring-grade steel
Initiate pricing pass-through discussions with top 20 OEM customers for Q2 contracts
Qualify Turkish flat steel suppliers (Erdemir, Kardemir) for Steyr and Bietigheim plants
Review inventory buffer policy for steel rings at Tongeren logistics hub

description: Houthi attacks on commercial shipping persist, forcing EU-Asia container traffic via Cape of Good Hope. Transit times increased 10-14 days, freight rates 2.5x above pre-crisis levels on Asia-Europe routes.
skf_exposure: Delays to components from China/India factories to European assembly. Nilai (Malaysia) to EU shipments +12 days. Bearing exports from Pune to EU customers affected.
recommended_action: Pre-position 2 additional weeks safety stock at Tongeren logistics hub. Shift urgent shipments to air freight for critical OEM deliveries.

actions.action: Pre-position 2 additional weeks of safety stock at Tongeren hub for Asia-sourced components
Shift critical OEM delivery shipments (automotive, wind) from Pune and Nilai to air freight
Negotiate volume discounts on Cape of Good Hope routing with Maersk and MSC
Evaluate rail-freight corridor via China-Europe (Chengdu to Duisburg) for Dalian/Wuhu output
Update customer lead times for Asia-sourced bearing lines by +10 days

event: Ukraine-Russia War — Lutsk Plant Operating Under Constraints
description: Ongoing conflict continues to affect SKF's Lutsk manufacturing facility in western Ukraine. Intermittent power outages and logistics challenges persist, though the plant remains operational.
skf_exposure: Lutsk bearing plant operating at ~70% capacity. Employee safety protocols active. Alternative supply from Chodov (Czech) and Karnare (Bulgaria) partially compensating.
recommended_action: Maintain dual-sourcing from Czech/Bulgaria plants. Continue employee support programs. Monitor escalation risk to western Ukraine.

actions.action: Continue dual-sourcing Lutsk production lines to Chodov (Czech) and Karnare (Bulgaria)
Maintain 4-week buffer stock of Lutsk-specific bearing SKUs at Tongeren
Review employee safety protocols and evacuation plans quarterly
Monitor frontline movement for escalation risk to Volyn Oblast (Lutsk region)

sources: ISW (Institute for the Study of War)

event: China Rare Earth Export Controls Expanded
description: Beijing expanded export licensing requirements for rare earth permanent magnets and processing technology. New controls affect samarium-cobalt and neodymium materials used in high-performance bearings.
skf_exposure: Sensor-bearing integrated units at Xinchang (SXC) and Yuyao (NGBC) plants use rare earth components. Supply for magnetic bearing lines at risk.
recommended_action: Qualify alternative magnet suppliers in Japan and Vietnam. Build 6-month buffer stock of critical rare earth components.

actions.action: Build 6-month buffer stock of samarium-cobalt and neodymium magnets at Xinchang and Yuyao
Qualify alternative magnet suppliers: Shin-Etsu (Japan) and Vietnam Rare Earth JSC
Assess magnetic bearing product lines for rare-earth reduction redesigns
Engage SKF Government Affairs for trade diplomacy with Chinese MOFCOM

description: Heavier than normal pre-monsoon rainfall affecting road infrastructure in Maharashtra. Pune-Mumbai expressway intermittent closures impacting container movement to JNPT port.
skf_exposure: Pune-Chakan factory export shipments delayed 2-4 days. Ahmedabad and Haridwar plants unaffected. Bangalore facility monitoring Karnataka rainfall levels.
recommended_action: Pre-ship Q3 export orders before peak monsoon. Coordinate with freight forwarders for alternative road routes.

actions.action: Pre-ship Q3 export orders from Pune-Chakan before peak monsoon onset (target: April 15)
Arrange alternative road routes via NH48 bypassing flooded expressway sections
Coordinate with JNPT port for priority container slots before monsoon peak
Monitor Karnataka rainfall levels for potential Bangalore plant impact

description: Dockworkers' union FNV in negotiations for new collective agreement. Previous rounds included work slowdowns. Key gateway for SKF European distribution.
skf_exposure: Rotterdam is primary import/export hub for SKF's European supply chain. Work stoppages would delay inbound steel and outbound finished bearings across EU.
recommended_action: Identify contingency routing through Antwerp and Hamburg. Pre-position critical inventory at Tongeren before negotiation deadline.

actions.action: Pre-position 3 weeks of critical bearing inventory at Tongeren hub before negotiation deadline (April 10)
Activate contingency routing agreements with Port of Antwerp and Port of Hamburg
Notify European OEM customers of potential 5-7 day delivery delays if strike occurs
Redirect inbound Asia shipments to Hamburg if Rotterdam work slowdown exceeds 48 hours

event: US Section 301 Tariffs on Chinese Bearings
description: USTR maintaining 25% tariffs on Chinese-origin bearings entering the US. No indication of relief in current review cycle. Chinese competitors absorbing partial cost, creating pricing pressure.
skf_exposure: Positive for SKF US manufacturing (Waukegan, Salt Lake City, Crossville) vs Chinese imports. But Dalian/Wuhu exports to US market face 25% duty.
recommended_action: Maximize US-origin production for domestic market. Route China production to non-US markets.

actions.action: Maximize production loading at Waukegan, Salt Lake City, and Crossville for US-market bearings
Reroute Dalian and Wuhu output to EMEA, APAC, and Mercosur markets instead of US
Evaluate Guadalupe NL (Mexico) capacity expansion for USMCA-origin production serving US
Monitor USTR review cycle for potential tariff changes in Q3 2026

description: PLA conducting increased naval exercises near Taiwan. Shipping insurance premiums for Taiwan Strait transit rose 15%. No direct trade disruption yet but contingency planning accelerating across industry.
skf_exposure: Taiwan offices (New Taipei, Taichung, Kaohsiung) manage semiconductor-bearing customer relationships. Strait closure would disrupt all East Asia maritime logistics.
recommended_action: Map Taiwan-dependent component flows. Prepare air-freight contingency for critical sensor components sourced from Taiwan.

actions.action: Map all Taiwan-dependent component flows including semiconductor-bearing sensor inputs
Pre-negotiate air-freight capacity with EVA Air Cargo and China Airlines for sensor components
Develop contingency plan to reroute East Asia maritime traffic south of Philippines if Strait closes
Brief executive team on Taiwan scenario impact: worst case 65K units/week across APAC plants
Identify alternative semiconductor-bearing sensor suppliers outside Taiwan (Japan, Korea candidates)

description: Argentine peso continued depreciation affecting Rosario plant cost competitiveness. Dollar-denominated input costs rising while local revenue partially hedged.
skf_exposure: Rosario manufacturing plant margins under pressure. Buenos Aires admin costs rising in USD terms. Export competitiveness improving for Mercosur customers.
recommended_action: Review hedging strategy for ARS exposure. Leverage improved export pricing to grow regional market share.

actions.action: Review and extend ARS/USD hedging strategy with Treasury for next 12 months
Leverage improved export pricing to grow lubrication system sales in Brazil and Chile
Negotiate USD-denominated contracts for key imported raw materials at Rosario
Evaluate shifting some Rosario production to export-focused lines to capture FX advantage

sources: Banco Central de la Republica Argentina

description: Industrial electricity prices in Germany stabilized at pre-2022 levels following new LNG terminal capacity. Positive for energy-intensive bearing manufacturing operations.
skf_exposure: Schweinfurt, Leverkusen, Heinsberg, Bietigheim plants benefit from normalized energy costs. Competitive position vs Asian imports improving.
recommended_action: Lock in favorable long-term energy contracts. Reinvest savings into automation at German facilities.

actions.action: Lock in 3-year fixed-rate industrial electricity contracts at current stabilized prices
Reinvest energy savings into automation upgrades at Schweinfurt and Bietigheim
Reassess total cost competitiveness of German plants vs Asian alternatives for EU-market bearings
Explore on-site solar/battery installations at Schweinfurt to further reduce energy cost baseline
